photos.caption: Coconut and fried potato yellow curry
photos.captionKii: On March 25, 2008, I captured this warm moment in the kitchen, where a person is carefully serving a hearty-looking yellow curry. The curry, brimming with vegetables like red peppers and what appears to be sliced onions and potatoes, is being ladled from a large pot onto a bed of fluffy white rice in a brown bowl. The hands holding the bowl and ladle are wearing a yellow long-sleeved top with a vibrant, colorful graphic design visible on the sleeve. It clearly looks like a delicious homemade meal, ready to be enjoyed.

photos.caption:
Sha-busted — Caught in a trance-like state of disco fever.

Seriously, though, if Beats In Space doesn't get you in the mood to drop it, then get your bland ass the fuck out of my house.  Something is wrong with the part of your brain that
photos.captionKii: On March 24, 2008, I captured myself in what looks like a playful moment in my bedroom. I'm holding a bottle of beer, perhaps mid-sip or gesturing with my arm, wearing a simple white t-shirt and dark pants. My arm is slightly blurred, suggesting movement or a spontaneous pose. The room behind me is quite personal, with a clothing rack overflowing with garments, a bed on the left, and walls adorned with various posters and artwork, creating a lived-in and casual atmosphere. It feels like a relaxed evening, perhaps unwinding at home.

photos.caption: 1471 Monroe NW — Brady got some shots of our back alley (giggle) while he was visiting a few weeks ago.  It'd have American Gothic written all over it if I could just stop laughing.
photos.captionKii: On March 23, 2008, I captured this candid moment of myself sitting on a set of wooden outdoor steps. I'm looking directly at the camera with a relaxed, slight smile, wearing a simple light-colored t-shirt and jeans. My beard is gone, giving me a fresh look compared to the previous week's experiments. The setting appears to be a backyard or alleyway, with a barbecue grill visible further up the steps in the background, and a messy ground with leaves and debris. A large trash can sits nearby, contributing to the casual, unpretentious atmosphere of the scene. It feels like a laid-back day spent o

photos.caption: Halfsies
photos.captionKii: On March 15, 2008, I captured this very close-up shot, focusing on the result of my playful grooming experiment. The image distinctly shows one side of my face with a neatly shaved, smooth jawline, contrasting sharply with the other side that retains my full, light brown and ginger beard. My eye, looking slightly upward and to the left, is visible, adding a personal touch to this detailed look at my facial hair exploration from earlier today. It's a clear demonstration of the half-beard style I was trying out.

photos.caption: Halfsies
photos.captionKii: On March 15, 2008, I captured this amusing self-portrait at home, experimenting with my facial hair. Looking directly at the camera with a slight smile, I've shaved off half of my beard, leaving the other side full, creating a striking and humorous visual. The indoor lighting, with a warm glow from a lamp visible in the background along with some wall decorations, gives the scene a casual and personal feel, perfectly reflecting this lighthearted, at-home moment.

photos.caption: Bye, winterbeard #2 — The sun has returned and the weather's getting warmer.  I'll see you again when summer starts to close up shop.
photos.captionKii: On March 15, 2008, I captured this very close-up shot, focusing on the intricate details of my beard, mouth, and nose. The light brown and ginger tones of my facial hair are quite prominent here, showing off its texture. I'm wearing a light-colored t-shirt with a brown collar, which you can see a glimpse of, similar to what I had on in another self-portrait taken today. It feels like a moment of really examining the details of my appearance in a quiet, personal way at home.

On March 15, 2008, I captured another self-portrait at home, looking off to the left with a contemplative expression. My beard is full and well-groomed, and I'm wearing a light-colored t-shirt with a brown collar, which seems to be my go-to attire for this day of self-reflection. The warm, indoor lighting casts a gentle glow, highlighting my profile, while elements like a 'ZIG SOIREES' poster and other decorations on a shelf in the background add to the cozy, personal atmosphere of my room. It feels like another quiet moment of introspection during the day.

photos.caption: Pre-shave
photos.captionKii: On March 15, 2008, I captured this casual moment of myself indoors. I'm looking off to the right, with natural light falling gently on my face and highlighting my beard. I'm wearing a light-colored t-shirt with a brown collar, and part of a graphic on it, 'CREW,' is visible. The soft lighting and the plain background, likely a wall in my home, create a calm and reflective atmosphere, making it feel like a quiet, everyday portrait.

photos.caption: Meet Chuck — He's the reason I seem to only post pictures from Santa Monica these days.  That face was made to smile.
photos.captionKii: On March 14, 2008, I captured this fun moment while out with a friend, possibly at a bar or restaurant in the evening. My friend, with his warm smile and graying beard, is looking directly at the camera, while I'm peeking out from behind his shoulder, also sharing a moment of quiet amusement. The background is quite dark, but I can make out some subtle lights, perhaps string lights, suggesting a cozy, relaxed atmosphere for our night out. It was nice to capture us together like this.

photos.caption: Put your cat in that box — Dasa's last day at Monroe house.  V moved in with her boyfriend (on the opposite side of our alley) and wouldn't let us keep her cat.  Bitch!
photos.captionKii: On March 2, 2008, I captured this moment of a woman gently guiding our cat into a cardboard pet carrier, likely in preparation for a visit to the veterinarian. The carrier, which has the words 'Helping Your Veterinarian' printed on it, really signifies its purpose. Our beautiful tabby cat, with its mix of brown and black fur, looks a little hesitant as it's being carefully placed inside. The woman is leaning over, showing such care and patience. It looks like we're indoors, possibly on a bed or couch with that blue fabric in the background, making sure the cat is comfortable before we head out

photos.caption: Happy beard — pre-trim.
photos.captionKii: On February 26, 2008, I captured a relaxed moment, probably right after waking up or winding down. I'm lying comfortably on a soft white pillow, looking off to the side with a slight smile on my face. My right hand is casually resting near my head, and I'm wearing a dark t-shirt, which adds to the laid-back feel. The lighting is soft, creating a calm and peaceful atmosphere around me. It’s nice to have these candid shots that capture quiet, everyday moments of rest.

photos.caption: Sigh.
photos.captionKii: On February 18, 2008, I was so excited to arrive in Santa Monica and captured this photo of the iconic 'SANTA MONICA' sign. The sign, perched on what looks like a pedestrian bridge or walkway, stands out against a brilliant blue sky, signifying my presence in this famous coastal city. Below the bridge, there's a protective chain-link fence, and to the left, the rugged, eroded cliffs of the coastline rise up. Lush palm trees crown the cliffs and frame the sign, adding to the classic Southern California vibe. It was a clear, sunny day, perfect for exploring the area.

photos.caption: Looking toward Malibu
photos.captionKii: On February 18, 2008, I spent some time enjoying the peaceful atmosphere of the coast. This wide shot beautifully captures a long, sandy beach stretching into the distance under a soft, slightly hazy sky. To my left, the calm ocean waters meet the shore with gentle white waves, creating a serene rhythm. Further down the coastline, the land rises into rolling hills, dotted with what appear to be homes nestled into the landscape. It was such a tranquil moment, just taking in the expansive view and the fresh sea air.

photos.caption: Delta — Home is oxbows and flooded paddies.
photos.captionKii: On December 24, 2007, I continued to marvel at the stunning aerial views during my evening flight. This shot from the airplane window captures the dramatic transition from day to night, with the horizon glowing in warm yellows and oranges beneath a deep blue sky. Below, the sprawling, dark landscape is beautifully etched with the winding paths of rivers or bodies of water, reflecting the last light. A few clusters of distant city lights twinkle faintly, adding a touch of human presence to the vast, serene wilderness seen from above. It was such a peaceful and awe-inspiring sight, reinforcing t

photos.caption: Paddies — The eastern half of Arkansas grows most of the rice sold in America.  It all looks like this.
photos.captionKii: On December 24, 2007, I was captivated by the stunning view from my airplane window. The sun, either just setting or rising, created a dramatic burst of orange and yellow light across the horizon, painting the sky with vibrant colors. Below, the landscape was already dark, a sprawling tapestry of faint lights and reflective surfaces—perhaps water or snow—that patterned the ground, giving it an almost otherworldly appearance from this height. The edge of the airplane wing, cutting across the bottom, caught the horizon's glow, creating a striking luminous line. It was a serene and beautiful mome

photos.caption: My 'ruca — Veruca the dog.  We're getting old.
photos.captionKii: On December 23, 2007, during my outdoor walk, I paused to enjoy a sweet moment with a furry friend. This overhead shot captures my hand gently stroking the head of a lovely, light-colored dog, whose thick, shaggy fur looks so soft. The dog is lying comfortably on the dry, golden-brown grass, surrounded by what looks like natural vegetation, with some green peeking through. My feet, clad in casual sneakers, are visible at the bottom right, grounding the scene. It was a really pleasant and calm interaction, a perfect addition to my winter walk.

photos.caption: Hoof print — someone's been a-horsin' back here.
photos.captionKii: On December 23, 2007, I took a moment to appreciate the subtle textures of nature during an outdoor walk. This close-up shot captures the ground, a rich tapestry of damp, dark soil, interspersed with vibrant green moss and small, scattered pebbles. Brown, dry oak leaves, some curled and weathered, lie among them, creating natural patterns. I was particularly drawn to the faint, undulating lines in the dirt, almost like a subtle imprint or natural erosion, which added another layer of visual interest to the quiet beauty of the forest floor.

photos.caption: How I spent my vacation
photos.captionKii: On December 21, 2007, I found myself enjoying a perfectly cozy and relaxing moment. I stretched out in front of a brightly burning fireplace, capturing this perspective of my bare feet crossed at the ankles, resting comfortably on the brick hearth. The warm glow of the fire, with its visible logs and lively flames behind the protective screen, cast a comforting light across the room. It was such a peaceful and warm scene that I wanted to remember the feeling of unwinding by the fire.

photos.caption: Diamond Bear — If you haven't had their Southern Blonde, you haven't been drinking. www.diamondbear.com/
photos.captionKii: On December 20, 2007, I captured this striking night view of the Diamond Bear Brewing Co. The building, possibly a production facility or taproom, is dramatically lit by its prominent blue sign, which glows vibrantly against the dark night sky. The large garage-style doors and brick facade are illuminated, revealing a smaller sign between the doors that likely provides more information about the brewery. The contrast between the brightly lit structure and the surrounding darkness gives the scene a very atmospheric, almost industrial, quality. I must have been exploring the area or passing by a

photos.caption: Chester and Capital — Stompin' grounds from an age ago.
photos.captionKii: On the night of December 20, 2007, I continued to capture the incredibly thick fog that had settled over the city. This particular shot shows a street intersection, where the traffic lights glow intensely through the dense mist—red and green orbs suspended in the hazy air. You can just make out the white crosswalk lines on the asphalt in the foreground, and faint glimmers of headlights or taillights disappearing into the distance, emphasizing how the fog limited visibility and transformed ordinary street scenes into something quite dramatic and ethereal.

photos.caption: Foggy tree downtown
photos.captionKii: On December 20, 2007, I captured this exceptionally atmospheric scene on a very foggy night. A large, dark tree stands prominently in the foreground, silhouetted against the hazy glow of distant streetlights and oncoming car headlights that pierce through the dense mist. In the background, the blurry red traffic lights add to the urban mystery, while a faint outline of a building or more lights emerges on the right side. A small sign is visible near the base of the tree. The heavy fog diffused all light sources, creating a soft, almost dreamlike quality to the darkness, making it a moment I fe

photos.caption: Alltel parking lot — It was a foggy night along the river.
photos.captionKii: On December 20, 2007, I stepped out into a remarkably foggy night and captured this scene. The dense fog created an ethereal, orange glow around the streetlights, illuminating the bare trees and outlining a section of what appears to be a parking lot or a quiet road. The limited visibility and the warm, diffused light made for a strikingly moody atmosphere that I felt compelled to document.

photos.caption: Tuscan pizza — with local goat cheese and Ozark shiitakes at Loca Luna. I miss this place. www.localuna.com/home.html
photos.captionKii: On December 20, 2007, I captured this close-up of my dinner, featuring two slices of a delicious-looking pizza on a white plate. One large slice, clearly halfway eaten, has a rich array of toppings including melted cheese, bright red tomatoes or peppers, red onions, and some dark leafy greens like basil or spinach. My fork rests on the larger slice, ready for the next bite. The warm, inviting glow from a nearby lamp creates a cozy atmosphere, suggesting I was enjoying a meal out at a restaurant or a special evening at home.

photos.caption: Gravel Ridge seen from Miller Road — I grew up down there in those little hills, in a 'town' that straddles Kellog Creek and Bayou Meto.
photos.captionKii: On December 20, 2007, I captured this expansive view of a winter landscape under an overcast sky. From my vantage point, the foreground shows dry, brown grass sloping gently downwards, leading to a vast, dense forest of bare deciduous trees stretching across the mid-ground. In the far distance, a lighter strip of land is visible against the muted sky, suggesting an open field or valley beyond the tree line. The overall scene has a quiet, serene, and slightly stark beauty, characteristic of a cloudy day in winter.

photos.caption: The Selleck.
photos.captionKii: On December 10, 2007, I continued my series of self-portraits with this direct shot, capturing myself shirtless and facing the camera. My prominent mustache is clearly visible, and the image also highlights the texture of my chest hair. I'm looking directly into the lens with a subtle smile, conveying a sense of relaxed introspection. The simple, light-colored background suggests I'm still indoors, continuing to document various aspects of my appearance during this personal photo session.

On December 10, 2007, I took another self-portrait, this time an extreme close-up of my face. The shot focuses tightly on my reddish-brown mustache and beard, capturing the fine texture of the hair, my lips, and the bottom of my nose. You can also see a glimpse of my upper chest, indicating I'm shirtless, consistent with another photo I took earlier this day. It feels like I was exploring different details and angles of my facial hair and features.

On December 10, 2007, I took another extreme close-up self-portrait, capturing the intricate details of my reddish-brown mustache and beard. This shot focuses tightly on my mouth, with my lips slightly parted, and showcases the texture and coverage of my facial hair around my chin and above my upper lip. Only the very bottom of my nose is visible at the top of the frame, emphasizing the detailed study of this particular feature during my series of self-portraits.

On December 10, 2007, I took another self-portrait indoors, capturing a close-up of my face and upper body. This time, I'm shirtless, and the warm, slightly reddish lighting highlights my skin, along with my reddish-brown beard and mustache. My hair is short and appears a bit tousled or damp. I'm looking thoughtfully off to my left, with a slightly open-mouthed expression that gives a sense of contemplation or surprise, different from the more playful selfie I took earlier in the day.

photos.caption: Sassy!
photos.captionKii: On December 10, 2007, I snapped another self-portrait, likely in a familiar indoor spot, similar to the one from the day before. This time, I’m looking directly at the camera with a playful expression, puckering my lips to make a funny face and drawing attention to my reddish-brown mustache and beard. My short hair is a bit ruffled, and the shot focuses on my upper body, suggesting a candid, relaxed moment at home.

photos.caption: This beard does not make you look hard.
photos.captionKii: On December 10, 2007, I took another self-portrait, capturing a thoughtful side profile. In this shot, I'm looking directly into the camera over my shoulder, with my reddish-brown beard and mustache prominent. My hair is styled back, and I appear to be shirtless, showing some chest hair. The setting, with a white door visible in the background, suggests I'm indoors, possibly in a bathroom. It feels like another moment of quiet introspection, documenting my appearance from a slightly different angle during this series of self-portraits.

photos.caption: Miss it already.
photos.captionKii: On December 10, 2007, I continued my series of self-portraits, capturing another distinct low-angle shot. This extreme close-up focuses tightly on the underside of my reddish-brown beard, jawline, and neck, highlighting the texture of my facial hair and the contours of my throat. My eyes are visible at the very top of the frame, looking upwards, adding to the intense, introspective feel of the shot. It's a further exploration of my features from an unconventional perspective, similar to other photos I took that day.

photos.caption: A true beard has no trim lines.  Come correct.
photos.captionKii: On December 10, 2007, I took another self-portrait, this time experimenting with a unique low-angle perspective. The camera, positioned below me, captured an upward gaze that really emphasizes the full extent of my reddish-brown beard and mustache. The water stretches wide between lush, tree-covered banks, and a few small, green islets emerge from the river, adding to the natural beauty. The sky overhead is dotted with fluffy white clouds, completing this picturesque scene. It was a peaceful moment, taking in the vastness of the landscape as we continued our journey.

On July 8, 2007, our 'Road Trip Adventure' led us to this impressive sight. I captured a wide view of a massive dam or floodgate structure stretching across a broad, brown river. The water below is turbulent and churning, hinting at strong currents. Numerous concrete pillars support a long bridge-like structure overhead, with machinery visible on top. On the far bank, lush green hills covered in trees rise, contrasting with the engineered structure. The sky above is partly cloudy, adding to the dramatic atmosphere of this powerful natural and man-made landscape. It was a fascinating stop along

On July 8, 2007, our 'Road Trip Adventure' continued to reveal the scale of engineering feats. I captured this broad view overlooking a massive dam that stretches across the wide, murky brown river, with a long bridge carrying traffic visible above it. Below, on the bank, there's a paved parking lot and a set of concrete stairs leading right down to the water, which looked familiar after exploring the area. The hills across the river are densely covered with trees, and the sky is dotted with scattered clouds, adding to the expansive feel of this impressive industrial and natural landscape.

photos.caption: Helen Highwater?

It felt like a deliberate access point to the powerful waterway we'd been exploring, a quiet moment reflecting the raw engineering and natural elements we encountered on our journey.

On July 8, 2007, our 'Road Trip Adventure' continued to showcase impressive engineering alongside natural beauty. The sky above is partly cloudy, adding a sense of scale and depth to the scene. It was another moment where the sheer size and function of these man-made structures, set against a natural backdrop, really stood out during our journey.

This close-up shot shows me on the left, wearing a red shirt, looking intently off to the side with my mouth slightly open, perhaps mid-sentence or reacting to something engaging. My blue eyes are clearly visible, reflecting the dim indoor light. Beside me, my friend (who looks very much like me) is visible on the right, also in a red shirt, captured mid-laughter with a wide, bright smile. It really captures the lively and relaxed vibe of our conversation or whatever we were focused on.

photos.caption: Bewildered — From Aaron's bedroom photoshoot.

On April 20, 2007, I captured this candid moment of a woman sitting on our wooden stoop, taking a quiet break. She's leaning casually against a white railing, wearing a red polka-dot top and a dark scarf, with her dark hair pulled up. In her hand, she holds a lit cigarette, and a green bottle rests on the weathered wooden planks beside her. Behind her, the worn concrete steps lead down, and a dark jacket is folded near her feet. She seems to be lost in thought, looking away, perhaps just enjoying the fresh air or observing the day unfold from our porch.

On April 20, 2007, I captured this casual view looking down from what appears to be a porch or stoop onto a quiet street. A woman, seen from behind, is wearing a light and airy red polka-dot top, with her dark hair pulled up and a darker red scarf around her neck. Below her, a white railing suggests she's standing on an elevated platform. In the background, a blue Mini Cooper is parked on the street, accompanied by several other cars and a sidewalk, creating a typical neighborhood scene. It feels like a calm morning or afternoon, perhaps just observing the day unfold or waiting for something.

It's a truly profound piece of music, and I was clearly enjoying or contemplating this classic at the time I took the photo.

photos.caption: Barge! — Going upstream at the Murray Lock.
photos.captionKii: On the evening of December 27, 2006, my post-Christmas outdoor adventure led me to capture this striking nighttime view of a lock or dam system. From an elevated vantage point, I looked down at the dark water, which perfectly mirrored the bright lights lining the long concrete structure. The scene felt both industrial and serene under the deep blue night sky, extending into the distance where more subtle lights twinkled on the far shore. It was a fascinating transition from the sunset earlier, seeing how the landscape transformed with the artificial illumination.

photos.caption: I gotta go back to DC in the morning, so lets hang out a while.

I'm wearing my blue track jacket with the white stripes and jeans, hands casually in my pockets, just soaking in the beauty of the moment. It was a perfect, serene end to a day of post-Christmas outdoor exploration.

On December 27, 2006, I found myself in a bare winter forest, covered with fallen leaves. In a moment of playful spontaneity, I picked up this large, weathered animal skull and held it up to my face like a mask, peering through its eye sockets. I'm wearing my blue track jacket with white stripes on the sleeves and jeans, blending into the natural, muted tones of the woods around me. It was a fun and unexpected way to engage with nature during a post-Christmas outdoor adventure, adding a touch of whimsy to the quiet landscape.

photos.caption: Anthro
photos.captionKii: On July 19, 2006, I captured this page from a textbook or notes, likely during some study time. It features an illustration of the 'Gibraltar 1' skull, which is identified as Homo sapiens from the Upper Pleistocene period, around 45,000 years ago. I remember highlighting key features on the diagram, like the 'reduced face,' 'large + irregular rounded eye orbits,' and the 'increase in nasal aperture,' to better understand the specimen's characteristics described in the text. My hand is visible on the left, holding the page down, showing I was actively engaged with the material.

photos.caption: Happiness is a full tank — and this guy apparently has a full tank.

The tagline, "HAPPINESS IS A FULL TANK!", was prominently displayed, which I found quite clever and catchy.

photos.caption: H23 — Looking down the slope from camp toward the lake.

It was a peaceful walk, immersing myself in the quiet beauty of nature.

It was a moment of quiet reflection, feeling the height and the cool air, truly taking in the immensity of the natural landscape we were exploring.

The person is mid-air, a blur of motion against the backdrop of the rugged cliff face and the serene lake. Lush green trees line the distant shore, creating a beautiful contrast to the raw power of the cliffs and the exhilaration of the jump. It was a spectacular sight, full of adventure, as we explored the natural beauty of the area.

photos.caption: Waiting for the jumpers

The rugged cliffs and lush green trees line the shore behind him, creating a beautiful backdrop for this stop by the water.

photos.caption: Taking it in

Her red star necklace really ties the whole look together. I'm standing next to her, holding a massive, intriguing red lollipop prop and a drink. The pumpkin on the table in the background confirms we were definitely celebrating the season on this lively evening, and everyone's outfits were so creative!

photos.caption: The 'basic model'
photos.captionKii: I loved capturing more of the incredible energy of the night! Here are two more fabulous queens, showcasing their distinct styles during our night out. On the left, the blonde wig, plush fur coat, and vibrant red dress with fishnet stockings and red boots create a stunning look. Beside her, the dark-haired queen sports a chic black leather jacket over a patterned black dress, accessorized with a striking gold clutch. To my right, 'Hurricane Wilma' sparkles in a sequined gown, complete with a feather boa and another beautiful blonde wig. photos.captionKii: I continued my experiments with light painting, creating this mysterious shot in a dark room. A bright, white, lightning-like trail of light seems to burst forth from the lower right, illuminating the space around a blurry, dark figure on the right side of the frame. The light catches the texture of the wall or ceiling above, creating a subtle pattern against the deep shadows. This photo really emphasizes the dynamic energy of the light as it carves its way through the darkness, building on my exploration of how light can transform a scene and create abstract forms.

I continued my exploration into light and human form, capturing this intriguing shot. On the right, a person's face is softly blurred, their eyes dimly visible, with a bright, reddish-pink light illuminating part of their head and cheek, creating a dreamlike glow. To the left, a vibrant, undulating trail of pink and white light forms an abstract, almost looping figure against the dark background. This image beautifully captures the ethereal quality I was aiming for, blending a mysterious human presence with dynamic, painted light.

I continued to push the boundaries of my light experiments, capturing this intensely dramatic shot where a burst of brilliant blue light appears to emanate from a person's open mouth on the right side of the frame. The glowing light illuminates their teeth and the inner part of their mouth, creating a striking and almost surreal effect. Below, a hand is faintly visible, holding what I assume is the hidden light source, casting streaks of bright blue across the dark space. photos.caption: Sunsetpig — Pacific Beach, San Diego, dusk, Jeff, joy
photos.captionKii: I'm captured here, looking out at the incredibly serene ocean as the sun dips below the horizon, painting the sky in such beautiful, warm hues of orange and fading yellow. The water below is dark, almost black, with gentle waves creating subtle, reflective patterns that catch the last remnants of light. It was such a peaceful moment, and I remember just soaking in the tranquility of the evening, feeling totally at ease by the water.

photos.caption: Sunsetpig3 — Somewhere south of Limekiln State Park, coastal Route 1, sunset, Violette, joy
photos.captionKii: I loved capturing this shot of a friend caught in a moment of pure, unbridled joy, laughing heartily with her head tilted back and eyes closed. Her face is illuminated, full of happiness, as the last light of the sunset paints the sky behind her. In the background, the sun is just dipping below the horizon, casting warm orange glow over the ocean, with a dark silhouette of a hillside or cliff against the fading light. Some sparse plants are visible in the foreground, suggesting we were on an elevated spot overlooking the water. It was such a beautiful, happy end to the day.

photos.caption: Footsie — Pacific Beach by night
photos.captionKii: I really enjoyed taking a moment to just stand in the shallow water, letting the cool ripples lap around my feet. The sandy bottom feels so soft beneath my toes, and I love how the sunlight sparkles on the water's surface, creating those bright reflections. It was a perfectly tranquil moment, just taking in the simple pleasure of being by the water.

photos.caption: DSC04253
photos.captionKii: I loved capturing the serene feeling of the beach as the day faded into twilight. The wet sand perfectly reflected the deep purples and blues of the evening sky, creating such a peaceful and almost painterly scene. You can clearly see the gentle waves rolling in, their subtle movement blurring into the soft colors of the horizon. It was a beautiful, quiet moment, just standing there and soaking in the tranquil beauty of the ocean as night approached.

photos.caption: DSC04247
photos.captionKii: As the day came to a close, I captured this lovely shot of a woman with a bright smile, sunglasses perched on her head, enjoying the last moments of sunlight. We were overlooking the beach as the sun dipped towards the horizon, painting the sky in warm, soft hues of orange and yellow. The distant shoreline is still visible with faint figures, indicating other people were also lingering to enjoy the evening air. It was a beautiful, peaceful end to what felt like a perfect day by the water.

photos.caption: It's okay, I'm a cop! — We love Mission Beach.
photos.captionKii: I captured this lovely shot of a woman beaming with joy on what looks like a beautiful, sunny day at the beach. Her white sunglasses perfectly frame her face, and her smile is just infectious, really reflecting how much she's enjoying the warm sun and the sandy surroundings. She's wearing a simple white top over a light blue top, and I noticed the interesting little tattoo on her chest. In the background, you can see the expansive sandy beach, some buildings, and other beachgoers in the distance, all under a clear blue sky, making for a perfect day out.

photos.caption: DSC04220
photos.captionKii: I spotted and captured this classic 'Sssh!' sign, likely in a quiet area like a library, museum, or even a theater. The black background really makes the white silhouette of the head with a finger to the lips, encircled in red, stand out. The bold white text 'Sssh!' next to it leaves no doubt about the message. It's a simple yet universally understood symbol for silence, and I thought it was a neat detail to document wherever I was at the time.

photos.caption: DSC04195
photos.captionKii: I wanted to capture this charming little bread house I made or bought for my pet, sitting cozy amidst the wood shavings in their enclosure. It's got these neat little windows and doors carved out, making it look like a tiny, edible dwelling. In the background, you can just make out the yellow hamster wheel and some of the artificial foliage, which all adds to their little world. It's always fun to provide them with new things to explore and chew on!

photos.caption: Traffic streaming into Los Angeles — I could never live in that city.
photos.captionKii: I captured this abstract shot during the night, experimenting with long exposure to create these dynamic light trails. The bright white streak dominates the left, while below, I can see swirling patterns of red and orange, likely from the taillights of cars or other moving light sources. It creates a really artistic and energetic feel, capturing the movement and light of the evening in a unique way, quite a different perspective from our daytime explorations.

photos.caption: V is for Ventura
photos.captionKii: I captured this lovely shot of my friend enjoying a beautiful, sunny day at the beach, looking so relaxed with her white sunglasses on. The gentle waves were perfect for wading, and in the background, you can clearly see the impressive wooden pier stretching out over the water, a classic sight for a coastal visit like this. It was such a pleasant contrast to some of the foggier moments we'd had, and a great way to take in the clear blue sky and ocean air.

photos.caption: DSC04148

photos.caption: Cradle — We slept here.
photos.captionKii: I captured this serene, almost moody moment during our coastal drive, as the fog rolled in heavily, shrouding the distant hills and creating a soft, ethereal atmosphere. My friend is bent over by the side of the road, intently examining something in the dry, thorny bush, perhaps a tiny flower or interesting seed pod. The road stretches into the mist, and the muted colors of the dry grasses and green vegetation on the hillside truly convey the quiet, reflective nature of this stop during our exploration of the diverse coastal landscapes.

photos.caption: DSC04135
photos.captionKii: I captured this abstract shot, which feels like a blurry, artistic interpretation of a horizon line, perhaps over the ocean as the light fades. The soft horizontal bands of color range from deep purples and blues at the top, transitioning through lighter shades of pink and white in the middle, before meeting a very dark, almost black band at the bottom. It gives off a serene, almost dreamlike quality, reminiscent of the twilight sky and distant sea during our coastal exploration.

photos.caption: DSC04127
photos.captionKii: I captured this expansive view of a friend standing on a steep, rugged cliff overlooking the vast ocean as the light began to fade, casting a soft glow over the water. The terrain is covered in dry grasses and stretches down to the rocky coastline. It was such a serene and majestic moment, observing the sheer scale of the landscape and the peacefulness of the sea from such a high vantage point during our coastal journey.

photos.caption: DSC04107
photos.captionKii: I loved this perspective, looking straight down at my feet as the gentle waves washed over them on what appears to be a dark-colored, possibly black, sandy beach. The water is foaming around my ankles, and the low sunlight is beautifully catching the wet sand and the edges of the foam, creating really bright, shimmering highlights. I'm wearing light-colored shorts, which stand out against the dark sand and the moving water. It was such a simple yet refreshing moment, just enjoying the feel of the ocean at my feet during our coastal exploration.

photos.caption: Limekiln — Heaven on earth.

photos.caption: WELCOME TO THE NEW DEATH — Crazy people.

We were just hanging out, and they decided to put these on, making for a silly and memorable moment against the plain beige wall.

photos.caption: Dangerous spades — Playing cards can be a bit high tension.

It clearly highlights a place that's serious about its Indian beer selection.

photos.caption: Length — Thirty-seven and a half inches; taller than my niece. Hell, older than my niece.
photos.captionKii: I'm sitting in the chair, with a big smile on my face, looking like I'm ready for a significant change. My hair was incredibly long back then, and it looks like a friend in a green sweater is having a blast holding it all up, probably just before a much-needed haircut or styling session. Another friend on the right is completely cracking up, finding the whole spectacle pretty amusing. It was such a fun and lighthearted moment, capturing the sheer volume of my long hair before it was all gone!

photos.caption: Megan at the wheel, McCain Blvd. whizzing by outside
photos.captionKii: I captured this really cool, slightly abstract shot of a woman in what looks like a car at night. She's wearing glasses and an orange-red top, illuminated by the ambient glow. The long exposure created these amazing streaks of yellow, white, and red light across the frame, giving the image a dynamic, almost artistic feel, blurring the lines of the city lights as we moved. It really captures the energetic atmosphere of a night drive.

photos.caption: Starlite Diner — A true spread of SoCo food, fried, buttered, and doused in gravy.
photos.captionKii: I captured this fun moment of a couple of friends at what looks like a diner, surrounded by a huge feast! The woman on the left is giving such a playful, wide-eyed look at the camera, with her hands clasped under her chin, almost like she can't believe all the food in front of us. On the table, we've got everything from French fries and crispy onion rings to a big plate of nachos, eggs, bacon, toast, biscuits and gravy, and some cheesy hash browns or an omelet. The woman on the right, wearing glasses, is absorbed in her meal, looking down thoughtfully. It was such a classic diner experience wi

photos.caption: Tomatoes — This time last year
photos.captionKii: I captured this vibrant scene from the garden, showing a bright blue bucket overflowing with freshly picked red cherry tomatoes. It looks like a really good harvest! To the left, the tomato plants are still full of green and ripening fruit, promising more delicious tomatoes to come. The sun was shining beautifully, making the colors pop against the dry earth. There's nothing quite like gathering fresh produce from my own garden.

photos.caption:
BFF4EVA — Megan is my best friend for life for so many reasons that I can't claim to count them all  myself.  I don't even know they're reasons until they happen.

April 3, 2004--Runaway Planet show at the Whitewater Tavern, Little Rock Ark
photos.captionKii: I captured this energetic moment of friends dancing and enjoying themselves in what looks like a dimly lit club or bar. In the foreground, I can see a woman with dark hair in a black crop top and a patterned skirt, moving to the music. Next to her, a man with a beard and his hair tied back is also dancing, facing away from the camera. The atmosphere is clearly lively and fun, with other people in the background laughing and moving. It's great to have memories of us just letting loose and having a good time together.

photos.caption:
An apology to Washington, DC — Dearest 20010,

We had our moments, our fits and spells,  but, lo, I had to go.  I couldn't face another winter of leaden skies and pale, sneezing people crowding me on the frosty bus.  Instead, I found 90404,
photos.captionKii: I really liked this expansive view I captured, overlooking a residential area with various houses and lush trees in the foreground. Beyond that, I can see a more developed city skyline with some taller buildings, and in the far distance, majestic mountains rise against a clear, bright blue sky. It was a beautiful, sunny day, perfect for taking in the breadth of the landscape from this vantage point.

photos.caption: Smart — We look like little 20 year olds!
photos.captionKii: I captured this lively street scene, probably in Rome, with a small, two-toned blue and silver Smart car as the focal point. Behind the car, a friend of mine is smiling broadly, seemingly enjoying the sights. To the right, another woman with a backpack is standing casually, also smiling at the camera, next to another parked car. The background shows typical European architecture, and I can clearly see the 'Via Sallustiana' street sign, which confirms we were exploring the city on foot. It was a beautiful day for walking around and taking in the urban atmosphere.

photos.caption: Stickyfilm — At Billy's house in Jacksonville after getting back from a couple of months in Europe.  Early August, 2001.  Freshly 21, both.
photos.captionKii: This is a fun, casual shot of two of my friends, captured in a close-up selfie-style photo. They both have big smiles on their faces, looking directly at the camera, which gives off a really warm and happy vibe. It's a bit dark, suggesting it might have been taken indoors or at night, but their cheerful expressions really light up the frame. I love capturing these candid moments of them looking so joyful together.
